ocr.text: The analysis of the data from Table 1 reveals various studies on anesthesia techniques and patient demographics. Mahadevan, D
(2012) involved 26 patients undergoing SNB and 26 patients undergoing LIA, with a gender distribution of 23 males and 29
females, a mean age of 67.2 years for SNB and 67.5 years for LIA, utilizing general anesthesia and standard medial parapatellar
surgery, administering 20 mL of 0.375% levobupivacaine for both FNB and LIA, and PCA for 48 hours for pain management.
Safa, B (2014) had 33 SNB and 32 LIA patients, with 32 males and 33 females, a mean age of 61.2 years for SNB and 60.7
years for LIA, used spinal anesthesia, and described no specified surgery method, also applying 20 mL of 0.5% ropivacaine for
FNB and LIA, and 50 mL of 0.2% ropivacaine for concomitant pain management. Tanikawa, H (2014) reported 23 SNB and 23
LIA patients, with gender distribution of 7 males and 39 females, mean ages of 72 years and 71 years, respectively, under
general anesthesia and standard mid-vastus approach, using 20 mL of 0.375% ropivacaine for FNB and LIA, with a complex
pain regimen.
Author and year  No of patients (SNB:LIA)  Gender male/female  Age (mean, SNB:LIA)  Anesthesia  Surgery method  FNB  The drug and doses of SNB  The drug and doses of LIA  Concomitant pain management

Safa, B 2014  33/32  32/33  61.2/60.7  Spinal anesthesia  NS  20 mL of 0.5% ropivacaine  20 mL of 0.5% ropivacaine  50 mL of 0.2% ropivacaine  PCA for 48 h

Tanikawa, H 2014  23/23  7/39  72/71  General anesthesia  Standard mid-vastus approach  20 ml of 0.375% ropivacaine  20 ml of 0.375% ropivacaine  200 mg of ropivacaine and 0.5 ml of adrenaline  0.2% ropivacaine infusion
Gi, E 2014  24/25  4/45  78/77  General anesthesia  NS  20 ml 0.375% ropivacaine  20 ml 0.375% ropivacaine  60 ml 0.5% ropivacaine with 0.3 mg epinephrine  60 mg oral loxoprofen sodium every 8 h

Nagafuchi, M 2015  17/16  5/28  72/73  General anesthesia  Midvastus approach  20 mL of 0.375% ropivacaine  20 mL of 0.375% ropivacaine  100 mL of 0.2% ropivacaine by adding 0.5 mL of adrenaline  Continuous femoral block of 0.2% ropivacaine

Uesugi, K 2014  105/105  41/159  76.3/76  Spinal anesthesia  Themid-vastus approach  20 mL of 0.75% ropivacaine  10 mL of 0.75% ropivacaine  20 ml of 0.75% ropivacaine, physiological saline 20 mL, adrenaline 0.3 mg, morphine hydrochloride and dexamethasone 3.3 mg  NS

Spangehl, M. J 2015  79/81  71/89  67.8/67.7  General anesthesia  Medial parapatellar approach  30 mL 0.5% ropivacaine  30 mL 0.5% ropivacaine  Ropivacaine Epinephrine Ketorolac Morphine sulfate  Analgesic medications
MicroRNA expression levels  GVHD grades (Number of patients)
miR-146a-5p  miR-155-5p  Grade 0  Grade I–III
The data continues similarly for Gi, E (2014), Nagafuchi, M (2015), Uesugi, K (2014), and Spangehl, M. J (2015). Table 2
illustrates microRNA expression levels related to GVHD grades, showing miR-146a-5p and miR-155-5p expression with
corresponding patient counts: ↑ for Grade 0 (8 patients) and Grade I–III (7 patients), ↓ for Grade 0 (5 patients) and Grade I–III
(10 patients), ↓ for Grade 0 (7 patients) and ↑ for Grade I–III (5 patients), and ↑ for Grade 0 (6 patients) and ↓ for Grade I–III (6
patients).
